name: Duchy of Courland and Semigallia (civil flag)
description: Divided into two square halves of red and white. The hoist half is a 2×2 chequer (red at upper-hoist and lower-fly, white at upper-fly and lower-hoist); the fly half is divided horizontally, red over white.
history: The civil ensign of the Duchy of Courland and Semigallia (1561–1795), the merchant flag of the Baltic duchy whose ships sailed under it during its 17th-century colonial ventures. The duchy was annexed by the Russian Empire in 1795.

features.symbolism: two square halves: a red-and-white chequer at the hoist and a red-over-white horizontal division at the fly, in the ducal colours of Courland
